q: Which ancient civilization built the Pyramids of Giza?
exp: The Pyramids of Giza were built about 4,500 years ago.

q: Who is credited with reaching the Americas in 1492?
exp: Columbus reached the Caribbean in 1492, sailing for Spain.

q: Which country built the Great Wall?
exp: The Great Wall of China is over 21,000 km long.

q: What were the warriors of old Japan called?
exp: Samurai followed a code of honour called bushido.

q: On which continent did Roman civilization begin?
exp: Rome began on the Italian peninsula, in Europe.

q: Which seafaring people sailed ships called drakkar?
exp: The Vikings came from Scandinavia and reached North America.

q: In which city stood the famous ancient Colosseum?
exp: Rome's Colosseum could hold up to 80,000 spectators.

q: What is the picture-writing of Ancient Egypt called?
exp: Hieroglyphs were decoded thanks to the Rosetta Stone.

q: Which material gives the 'Stone Age' its name?
exp: In the Stone Age, tools were made mostly of chipped stone.

q: Which explorer led the first voyage around the world?
exp: Magellan set off in 1519; the expedition finished the loop in 1522.

q: Which empire was ruled by emperors like Julius Caesar and Augustus?
exp: Augustus became the first Roman emperor in 27 BC.

q: Which ancient wonder still standing is in Egypt?
exp: The Great Pyramid is the only Ancient Wonder still largely intact.
